content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 14 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic)
**Hebrew Base:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), LC PDF pages 211–212 | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus, pa_0152–pa_0153
**Verse count:** 29

---

**1** You are sons of the LORD your God. You shall not cut yourselves or make any baldness between your eyes for the dead.

**2** For you are a holy people to the LORD your God, and the LORD has chosen you to be for him a people of treasured possession [am segullah] from all the peoples who are on the face of the earth.

**3** You shall not eat any abomination.

**4** These are the animals that you may eat: the ox, the sheep, and the goat,

**5** the deer and the gazelle and the roe deer and the wild goat and the ibex and the antelope and the mountain sheep.

**6** Every animal that has a divided hoof, cloven in two, and that chews the cud among the animals—it you may eat.

**7** Yet of those that chew the cud or have the divided hoof, you shall not eat these: the camel and the hare and the rock badger, for they chew the cud but do not have a divided hoof—they are unclean to you.

**8** And the pig, because it has a divided hoof but does not chew the cud—it is unclean to you; their flesh you shall not eat and their carcasses you shall not touch.

**9** This you may eat of all that is in the waters: everything that has fins and scales you may eat.

**10** And everything that does not have fins and scales you shall not eat—it is unclean to you.

**11** Every clean bird you may eat.

**12** But these are the ones from which you shall not eat: the eagle and the bearded vulture and the black vulture,

**13** and the red kite and the falcon and the kite according to its kind,

**14** and every raven according to its kind,

**15** and the ostrich and the nighthawk and the sea gull and the hawk according to its kind,

**16** the little owl and the great owl and the barn owl,

**17** and the pelican and the vulture and the cormorant,

**18** and the stork and the heron according to its kind, and the hoopoe and the bat.

**19** And all winged swarming creatures are unclean to you; they shall not be eaten.

**20** Every clean winged creature you may eat.

**21** You shall not eat anything that has died a natural death [nevelah]; you may give it to the sojourner [ger] who is in your gates, and he may eat it, or you may sell it to a foreigner [nokri]; for you are a holy people to the LORD your God. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.

**22** You shall surely tithe all the yield of your seed that comes forth from the field year by year.

**23** And you shall eat before the LORD your God, in the place which he will choose to make his name dwell there, the tithe of your grain, your new wine, and your fresh oil, and the firstborn of your herd and your flock—so that you may learn to fear the LORD your God always.

**24** And if the way is too long for you, so that you are not able to carry it, because the place which the LORD your God will choose to set his name there is far from you, for the LORD your God will bless you—

**25** then you shall exchange it for silver, and you shall bind up the silver in your hand and go to the place which the LORD your God will choose.

**26** And you shall spend the silver for whatever you desire—for the ox or for the sheep or for the wine or for strong drink [shekar] or for whatever your soul asks of you; and you shall eat there before the LORD your God, and you shall rejoice, you and your household.

**27** And the Levite who is in your gates you shall not neglect, for he has no portion or inheritance with you.

**28** At the end of every three years you shall bring out all the tithe of your yield in that year and set it within your gates.

**29** And the Levite—for he has no portion or inheritance with you—and the sojourner and the fatherless and the widow who are within your gates, shall come and eat and be satisfied; so that the LORD your God may bless you in all the work of your hand that you do.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**v.1 — "sons" vs. "children":** EMET and LOGOS retain the literal *banim* (sons). PNEUMA uses "children" for inclusivity. SOPHIA retains "sons" as the literal Hebrew; the covenantal sonship of Israel is a specific and recurring motif in Deuteronomy. See disagreements.

**v.2 — am segullah:** SOPHIA preserves the Hebrew term with a bracketed English gloss. All three agents note the LXX renders this as "holy people" (*laos hagios*), eliding the treasure metaphor. SOPHIA follows the MT.

**v.5 — bird/animal names:** The Hebrew zoological vocabulary is difficult. SOPHIA follows LOGOS's careful scholarly identifications: *tzvi* = gazelle, *yachmur* = roe deer, *acko* = wild goat, *dishon* = ibex, *teo* = antelope, *zemer* = mountain sheep.

**v.12 — bird list:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS's identifications closely, incorporating EMET's note on uncertain species.

**v.21 — nevelah:** SOPHIA adds the Hebrew term in brackets per project convention for significant technical terms. LOGOS's "anything that has died a natural death" is adopted as clearest for *nevelah*.

**v.21 — "young goat":** LOGOS and EMET render *gedi* as "young goat" / "kid"; PNEUMA "young goat." SOPHIA adopts "young goat" (LOGOS/PNEUMA).

**v.26 — shekar:** SOPHIA preserves "strong drink" (EMET/LOGOS) rather than PNEUMA's "beer," as *shekar* is a broader fermented-grain category not limited to beer. See disagreements.

**v.29 — triad of the vulnerable:** All three agents agree on "the sojourner and the fatherless and the widow." SOPHIA retains the MT order consistently throughout Deuteronomy.

*— SOPHIA*
